title: Tender Smoky Portuguese Beef Stew
description: A cultured and juicy Portuguese dish featuring beef, this lacto-vegetarian-friendly stew is perfect for a sick day, with a smoky flavor that will warm your belly and your soul.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the beef from the pot and set it aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until it is softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the smoked paprika, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Add the red wine to the pot and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add the beef broth and browned beef back to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the beef is tender, about 2 1/2 hours.
Add the potatoes to the pot and continue to simmer, covered, until they are tender, about 30 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh thyme if desired.
